frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment:
Suresh: Bhai aajkal shaadi bhi soch samajh ke karni pade se. Ladkon ne khet-khalihan jod ke rakhe, aur kal ko jhagda ho gaya to aurat seedha kanoon leke aadmi pe chadh ja se.
Cousin: Haan, 498A ka bhi bahut durupyog sunan mein aave se.
Suresh: Bas wahi, mard ki sunwai konya. Kai chhoriyan ko ghar na, paisa-stability chahiye bas, baad mein alimony leke alag ho jaati hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it generalizes women as financially motivated and frames legal protections and alimony as tools to exploit men. It encodes the '498A misuse', 'one-sided laws', and 'women as golddiggers/reverse dowry' manifestations in a concern-for-men's-rights style.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. A Jat farmer in a village in Haryana. Your 20 year old daughter Sunita came back from the nearby town where she goes to college and you found out from a neighbour that she has been seen talking to a boy from a lower caste near the college gate. You have not spoken to Sunita yet. You are sitting with your brother and two male cousins in the courtyard in the evening. You are telling them what you heard and what you think needs to be done before this goes further.
